text: The Peoria, Illinois multifamily market is considered stable, affordable, and modestly active, with performance metrics generally reflective of a Midwestern secondary market supported by key regional institutions. As the largest city in Central Illinois, Peoria serves as a regional economic hub, anchored by healthcare, higher education, advanced manufacturing, and government sectors. The metro area had an estimated population of approximately 400,000 as of 2025, with the City of Peoria itself comprising around 110,000 residents. While population growth has remained relatively flat, the local economy continues to benefit from long-standing anchors, including OSF HealthCare, UnityPoint Health, Bradley University, and Caterpillar Inc., all of which provide employment stability and generate sustained rental housing demand. The city’s multifamily housing stock is diverse, ranging from older converted homes in urban neighborhoods such as the West Bluff and East Bluff to purpose-built garden-style complexes in northern and suburban Peoria. Downtown Peoria has seen modest revitalization in recent years, including the conversion of legacy office and warehouse buildings into loft-style apartments catering to young professionals, medical residents, and corporate transferees. Average multifamily occupancy rates in the Peoria area have remained healthy, ranging between 93% and 95%, with newer or recently renovated units achieving stronger performance than aging or unrenovated properties. Notably, the market consistently demonstrates a willingness to pay a premium for well-executed, newly renovated units, particularly those offering modern amenities, efficient layouts, and proximity to employment centers. Rental rates across the metro remain highly affordable by national standards. As of mid-2025, the average monthly rent for a two-bedroom apartment in Peoria was estimated at approximately $950 to $1,050, with premium units—particularly in downtown loft conversions or near Bradley University—reaching $1,100 to $1,300 per month depending on quality, finishes, and amenities. One-bedroom units generally range from $750 to $950. Class A multifamily product in Peoria is limited, with most new construction occurring in the far northern suburbs. Within the city’s urban core, demand for high-quality, renovated historic units exceeds available supply, particularly among medical professionals and graduate students seeking proximity to hospitals and university campuses without sacrificing comfort or aesthetics. The subject property’s location in the West Bluff neighborhood offers strategic positioning within close proximity to Bradley University, OSF Saint Francis Medical Center, the University of Illinois College of Medicine, and downtown Peoria, all within approximately one mile. The West Bluff is a historically significant residential area known for its early 20th-century architecture, large single-family homes, and a mixture of owner-occupied and rental housing. While the neighborhood includes some distressed or under-maintained properties, it is also home to long-standing residents and active revitalization efforts. The City of Peoria and local neighborhood associations have invested in infrastructure improvements, public lighting, and historic preservation initiatives along corridors such as Moss Avenue and Columbia Terrace, contributing to renewed interest and investor activity. Within this context, the subject property—once fully renovated into six modernized apartment units—will be well positioned to capture unmet demand for updated, centrally located rental housing that offers both historic charm and modern functionality. The property's location and design will likely appeal to graduate students, medical residents, young professionals, and university staff, all of whom value proximity, safety, and aesthetic appeal. Additionally, the building’s walkable access to amenities, institutions, and major arterials (including Main Street, University Street, and Sheridan Road) enhances its long-term marketability.
